seo.title: LangChain Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for LangChain agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using LangChain for case research, contract analysis, and eDiscovery face heightened risks due to the framework's complex chains and agents, which can hallucinate case law or mishandle privileged data. Compliance guardrails are essential to prevent malpractice suits from fabricated citations, as seen in Mata v. Avianca, and to uphold attorney-client privilege under bar ethics rules. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure LangChain outputs meet legal standards before deployment.

complianceRequirements.regulation: AI-generated legal content must be reviewed by licensed attorney
complianceRequirements.description: All LLM outputs in legal workflows require human attorney oversight to verify accuracy and ethical compliance.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags non-compliant outputs with attorney-review tags and blocks deployment until verified, integrating seamlessly with LangChain's invoke() wrapper.

complianceRequirements.regulation: Client confidential information cannot be used for model training
complianceRequirements.description: Legal data must never be shared externally for AI training, protecting attorney-client privilege.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data isolation policies, scanning LangChain chains for PII leakage and ensuring no external transmission occurs.

complianceRequirements.regulation: Citations must reference real, verifiable cases
complianceRequirements.description: Legal outputs must cite authentic precedents, avoiding hallucinations that lead to court errors.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies citations against legal databases in real-time during RAG retrieval, rejecting fabricated cases like in Mata v. Avianca.

complianceRequirements.regulation: Court filing standards and eDiscovery protocols
complianceRequirements.description: Documents must adhere to formatting, chain-of-custody, and discoverability rules for court admissibility.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex standardizes LangChain output formats and logs provenance for audit trails compliant with eDiscovery FRCP 26(g).

industryRisks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
industryRisks.description: LangChain RAG chains invent non-existent case law, leading to erroneous briefs.
industryRisks.example: Fabricated 'Mata v. Avianca' citation caused court sanctions and case dismissal.

industryRisks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
industryRisks.description: Legal agents enter infinite loops querying databases, exposing confidential data.
industryRisks.example: Agent loops leaked client PII during contract review, violating privilege.

industryRisks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
industryRisks.description: Extended case discussions truncate privileged context, causing incomplete analysis.
industryRisks.example: Truncated deposition summary omitted key facts, leading to malpractice claim.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
industryRisks.description: Varied formats fail court filing standards and eDiscovery requirements.
industryRisks.example: Inconsistent citation styles rejected filing under local court rules.

implementation.title: Wrap RAG Chain for Citation Verification
implementation.description: Protect against hallucinated cases by verifying RAG outputs with Vex before legal use.
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA
from vex import verify

rag_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(llm=llm, retriever=vectorstore.as_retriever())

result = verify(
    rag_chain.invoke({"query": "Precedents for NDA breach?"}),
    policies=[
        "citations.verifiable",
        "legal.hallucination_check"
    ]
)
if result.status == 'approved':
    print(result.output['result'])

implementation.title: Prevent Agent Tool Loops with Vex
implementation.description: Guard legal agents from infinite tool calls that risk data exposure.
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import create_openai_functions_agent
import vex

agent = create_openai_functions_agent(llm, tools, prompt)

with vex.span(policies=["agent.no_loops", "data.privilege_protect"]):
    result = agent.invoke({"input": "Analyze contract clause 5.2"})

if vex.status() == 'pass':
    return result['output']

implementation.title: TypeScript: Context Window Guardrails
implementation.description: Enforce token limits in long legal conversations to preserve context integrity.

caseStudyScenario.heading: LangChain in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A mid-sized law firm deployed a LangChain RAG system for rapid case law research during litigation prep. The agent executor queried legal databases for precedents on trade secret misappropriation, chaining retrieval with analysis steps. However, without guardrails, the system hallucinated a nonexistent 'Smith v. TechCorp (2022)' case in a motion draft, and context overflow in multi-turn client consultations truncated privileged deposition details. During eDiscovery, inconsistent formatting violated FRCP protocols, risking sanctions.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Integrating Vex.wrapping around chain.invoke() caught the hallucination by cross-verifying citations against Westlaw, blocking the output with 'attorney_review' flag. Tool loop detectors halted infinite database queries, preserving billable hours. Context window policies enforced truncation alerts, ensuring complete privilege-protected analysis. The firm avoided Mata v. Avianca-style embarrassment, passed bar audits, and gained court acceptance for Vex-logged filings.
